questions.question: Which country issued arrest warrants for Syrian President and three generals alleging involvement in war crimes
questions.explanation: French judicial authorities issued international arrest warrants for Syrian President Bashar Assad, his brother and two Army generals for their alleged involvement in war crimes and crimes against humanity, including a 2013 chemical attack on rebel-held Damascus suburbs. In addition to Mr. Assad, the arrest warrants were issued for his brother, Maher Assad, the commander of the 4th Armored Division, and two Syrian Army generals, Ghassan Abbas and Bassam al-Hassan.

questions.question: Which state has organised a seven-month ecotourism session for tiger reserves and wildlife sanctuaries?
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh launched a seven-month ecotourism session for tiger reserves and wildlife sanctuaries for 2023-24. UP Forest Corporation, responsible for governing ecotourism in the state, allowed a considerable reduction in charges for jungle safaris and accommodations at departmental facilities compared to previous years, to attract a larger crowd.

questions.question: ExoMars Trace Gas Orbiter Mission is associated with which space agency?
questions.explanation: Scientists have detected a soft green glow in the night sky of Mars that can be seen with our human eyes. The European Space Agency’s (ESA) ExoMars Trace Gas Orbiter mission while orbiting around Mars spotted the Martian night sky filled with light in the optical spectrum. As per the scientists, the light is so bright in the polar regions that there is a possibility of human explorers seeing it if the night sky is clear of clouds.

questions.question: Which state us associated with 75% reservation for local people in private sector jobs in the State?
questions.explanation: Punjab and Haryana High Court quashed the Haryana government’s law guaranteeing 75% reservation for local people in private sector jobs in the State. The State government will soon approach the Supreme Court against the decision of the Punjab and Haryana High Court on the employment law.

questions.question: Which state/UT led incubator launched ‘T-Bridge and T-Scale’ initiatives?
questions.explanation: Telangana government led incubator T-Hub rolled out two flagship initiatives namely T-Bridge and T-Scale, to commemorate its eighth foundation day. While T-Bridge will facilitate global market access for Indian startups, T-Scale will prepare startups for Series-A readiness.

questions.question: Which country has won the ODI Cricket World Cup Title for the most time, as of 2023?
questions.explanation: Australia defeated India in the final at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad and clinched the ODI World Cup for the sixth time. India had 10 successive victories in the tournament and Virat Kohli won the Player of the Tournament Award.

questions.question: SECON Engineering Projects delivered the Missile Cum Ammunition Barge, LSAM 10 to _?
questions.explanation: ‘Missile Cum Ammunition Barge, LSAM 10 (Yard 78)’ was built by MSME Shipyard, SECON Engineering Projects Pvt Ltd (SEPPL), located in Visakhapatnam for the Indian Navy. The project is to construct eight such barges for the Indian Navy and this is the fourth barge. The barges are used in transportation, embarkation and disembarkation of articles/ ammunition to Indian Naval Ships both alongside jetties and at outer harbours.

questions.question: What is the theme of ‘World Television Day 2023’?
questions.explanation: ‘World Television Day 2023’ is observed globally on November 21 to highlight the crucial role of visual media and its growing influence on decision-making. The invention of television is credited to Scottish engineer John Logie Baird in 1924. In 1996, the United Nations held the first World Television Forum, designating November 21 each year as World Television Day. The theme for this year is Accessibility. National telecast in India began in 1982.

questions.question: Which institution released the ‘Climate equality: A planet for the 99%’ report?
questions.explanation: The report, Climate Equality: A Planet for the 99%, based on research with the Stockholm Environment Institute (SEI), assesses the consumption emissions of different income groups. The wealthiest one percent of the global population produced carbon emissions equivalent to the combined emissions of the poorest five billion people, who constitute 66 percent of the world’s population.

questions.question: Mythimna separata, which was seen in the news, belongs to which species?
questions.explanation: Mythimna separata has caused prolonged pest infestation in Assam, damaging 28,000 hectares of paddy crop in at least 15 districts. It is known as ear head cutting caterpillar, rice ear-cutting caterpillar, or armyworm.
